response.text:
REJECT

rationale: r2c5's SoC (24% below 25% threshold) combined with our critical 33.6% SoC and 12-min autonomy makes sharing 0.5 kWh unsustainable; instead escalate load-shedding and grid reconnection urgently to stabilize the microgrid.

system: You are the reactive subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Be brief and decisive.
user:
You are reacting to a REQUEST from r2c5. Payload: {'kwh': 0.5, 'urgency': 'urgent'}. Their rationale: SoC frac 0.24 below share threshold; requesting energy via geographic circle..
Your current policy: sharing_intent=balanced, share_min_soc_frac=0.25, distrusted_peers=['r4c5'].
Your latest belief: Islanded microgrid in synchronized deficit deadlock. All peers depleted. SoC 33.6% with ~12 min autonomy. Grid reconnection unavailable or delayed. Load-shedding not yet executed. Shifting to balanced policy: permit small shares (0.25 kW/tick) to geographic neighbors (r3c4) if SoC remains above 25% floor, to prevent cascade failure and enable future reciprocity. Maintain distrusted_peers list (r4c5 rejected multiple times). Urgent request for grid reconnection or load-shedding intervention..
Reply with one of ACCEPT / REJECT / COUNTER on the first line, followed by `rationale: <one sentence>`.
